mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate lower the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, labor market slack reduces corporate profit margins and consumer spending growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts signal a broad contraction in consumer demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative returns with positive return frequency exceeding 55%, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month increases in the industrial production index during periods of rising inflation indicate supply-side overheating, prompting expectations of tighter monetary policy and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth during high inflation periods yields positive SPX returns in greater than 60% of cases, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in CPIAUCSL crossing above 0.4% signals accelerating price pressures that compress real equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in greater than 60% of months where CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.4%, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate consumer spending and corporate revenue forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive SPX returns with positive return frequency below 50%, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over 3-year rolling periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 3.0% during periods of low unemployment indicates an overheating economy that forces the Federal Reserve to raise rates, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: High GDP growth during low unemployment periods yields positive SPX returns in greater than 60% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE falls below 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling payroll employment signals productivity-driven supply expansion that boosts corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-PAYEMS divergence fails to predict positive returns with positive return frequency below 50%, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant in walk-forward testing.
